self_correction_logic: Q1. Wait, did I check if the sample consists of students? Yes, the sample consists of NPD managers, not students. Q2. Wait, did I check the correlation matrix N to ensure it represents individuals, not teams or firms? Yes, the unit of analysis is explicitly stated as N=189 product development projects/plants, not individuals. Q3. Wait, is the boundary spanning being performed by the focal respondent, or are they rating their manager? The focal respondent (manager) acts as a key informant rating the project team's/firm's collaborative efforts. Q4. Wait, did they measure mere communication frequency or adaptive selling instead of purposive boundary spanning? No, they measured project-level collaboration. Q5. Wait, is the BSB variable just a moderator or correlated with regression residuals? No. Q6. Wait, is this Leader BSB (subordinates rating leader's boundary spanning)? No. Q7. Wait, is this Intra-Organizational BSB (cross-departmental, cross-functional)? Although the paper measures cross-functional involvement, it is assessed at the project team level rather than as an individual's interpersonal behavior. Q8. Wait, before I apply Code 1, is the construct actually BSB (just measured differently)? The construct evaluates macro-level (project/plant) collaboration. Thus, the study violates the individual-level criteria and must be excluded under Code 3. No multiple flaws, just Code 3.
reason_summary: Non-individual level analysis (Project/Plant-level key informant data)
verbatim: [Section 4.1. Sample] "To get accurate responses, the product development manager was asked to refer to a 'recent major product development project' in which he/she participated, thus making a product development project the unit of analysis in this study." [Section 4.1. Sample] "The final data set consists of 189 responses from product development managers relating one project to one plant." [Appendix A. Measures] "SUPINV2 We partnered with suppliers for the design of this product." [Appendix A. Measures] "CUSTINV1 We consulted customers early in the design efforts for this product." [Appendix A. Measures] "CRFINV1 New product design teams have frequent interaction with the manufacturing function."
